ogTitle: Grange vs Green Hills Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Grange and Green Hills Range.
heading: Grange vs Green Hills Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Grange, South Australia and Green Hills Range, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Green Hills Range has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Grange (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Grange or Green Hills Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Grange has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Green Hills Range scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Grange scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Green Hills Range scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Grange or Green Hills Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Green Hills Range has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
